instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Do You Get Money Back After Term Life Insurance?
Term insurance plans are protection-oriented plans that are known to pay no maturity benefit. However, this is not always the case. If you need a maturity benefit along with a comprehensive scope of protection, there are term plan options that you can explore. So, let’s understand.
Can You Get Money Back After Term Insurance?
Yes, you can. The return of premium term insurance plan,  is a type of term plan that returns you the premium on maturity.
As such, with the return of premium term insurance plans, you get your money back when the plan matures, and you survive the policy term, subject to policy terms & conditions.
Let’s analyse both types of term plans, one that doesn’t pay any money back and the other that does.
Type 1: Term Policies That Do Not Offer Money-Back - Basic Term Insurance
A basic term insurance plan is one that covers only death risk during the term of the policy. It does not pay any benefit if you survive the policy term and the plan matures.
The benefits of a basic term insurance Policy are:
The life Cover.
You can opt for a high sum assured at pocket-friendly premium rates. Moreover, modern-day term plans allow comprehensive coverage with inbuilt riders (at nominal extra cost) and the option to avail of whole life coverage.
Premium payment flexibility
The premium of the policy can be paid in a single instalment, for a limited tenure or throughout the policy tenure, depending on your suitability. Thus, you get flexible premium payment tenures that help you afford the term plan without any hassle.
You can save on tax
The premiums paid for the policy qualify for a deduction under Section 80C up to Rs.1.5 lakhs under Income Tax Act, 1961. So, you can reduce your tax liability with term insurance premiums and save tax subject to provisions stated in the Act.
You can tune the coverage as per your needs by adding optional riders
Optional riders are available under basic term plans. You can choose one or more riders on payment of nominal additional premium and adjust the coverage as per your needs.
Type 2: Term Policies That Offer Money-Back
Term insurance With Return of Premium policies are term plans that return the premium when the policy matures..
The benefits of Term Plans with Return of Premium option  are as follows –
Premium return
The primary benefit of a Term insurance With Return of Premium s the maturity benefit. The aggregate premium paid during the policy tenure is returned back if no death benefit is paid during the policy term. As such, you enjoy the financial security of insurance protection, failing which you get back the premiums after the policy tenure,subject to terms & conditions of the policy.
Optional riders
There are optional riders available with the policy. You can choose to add the riders to the coverage on payment of nominal premium and enhance it for an all-inclusive scope of protection. On maturity, however, the rider premium is not refunded back.
Tax benefits
Like basic term plans, the premium paid for such policies are also eligible for a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions state therein. You get a deduction up to a sum of Rs.1.5 lakhs. Also, the maturity benefit that you get is considered to be a tax-free income in your hands under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions state therein..
Term Insurance Premium Calculator
Both basic term insurance plan and Term insurance With Return of Premium  plans have affordable premiums since they aim to secure you against the risk of premature death. You can also calculate the expected premium of these policies using a term insurance premium calculator.
A calculator is an online tool that helps you find the estimated premium of the policy based on the details that you enter into the calculator, i.e., your age, the sum assured, tenure, etc.
How does term insurance with a return of premium work?
Under a Term Insurance with Return of Premium option, you choose the sum assured, policy tenure and premium paying term and frequency. Based on these details, your age, medical condition, etc., the premium of the policy is calculated.
You pay the premium through the tenure that you choose. In the case of death during the policy tenure, the sum assured would be paid, provided all due premium are paid, and the policy would be terminated. On the other hand, if you survive the policy tenure, the total premium that you paid during the policy term (excluding GST and the rider premium paid if any) would be refunded back in a lump sum.
Let’s understand with an example –
Mr Rakesh buys a term plan with a return of premium option for a sum assured of Rs.20 lakhs. He chooses a term of 20 years and pays regular annual premiums throughout the policy tenure. The annual premium works out to Rs.25,000.
Scenario 1 - Mr Rakesh dies during the policy term
In this case, the sum assured of Rs.20 lakhs would be paid to the nominee as a death benefit. After the payment of the death benefit, the coverage would be terminated.
Scenario 2: Mr Rakesh survives until the end of the policy term
If Mr Rakesh survives for 20 years and the policy matures, the premium will be returned. Over the tenure, Mr Rakesh paid a total premium of Rs.5 lakhs (Rs.25,000 * 20). Thus, on maturity, Rs.5 lakhs would be refunded back to him, and the policy would be terminated.
Getting Your Money Back Within the Free-Look Period
There’s a free-look period in term insurance plans, in both basic and Term insurance With Return of Premium option  The period is 15 days from the date of receipt of the policy bond by the policyholder. In the case of electronic policies and policies sourced through distance marketing mode, the period is 30 days.
The free-look period allows you to cancel the coverage if you are not satisfied with the terms and conditions of the policy. If, after buying the policy, you see that the plan is not what you expected, you can cancel the plan and seek a premium refund during the freelook period.
Cancellation of policy during the free-look period allows a refund of the premium. the entire premium is refunded back after deducting the following expenses –
any expenses incurred by the insurer on medical examination of the insured persons and the stamp duty charges or;
where the risk has already commenced and the option of cancellation of the policy is exercised by the policyholder, a deduction towards the proportionate risk premium for period of cover or;
Where only a part of the insurance coverage has commenced, such proportionate premium commensurate with the insurance coverage during such period
Final Word
If you seek the benefit of comprehensive coverage along with a maturity benefit, you can opt for a return of premium term insurance plans. Such plans give you your money back if the plan matures and you survive till the maturity date, and all due premiums are paid.
So, assess your needs and then consider investing in a basic term plan or a Term Plan with Return of Premium option  to enjoy financial security.
FAQs
If I outlive the term of the term plan, would I be eligible to get a maturity benefit?
If you have opted for a basic term insurance plan, you will not get any maturity benefit if you outlive the policy tenure. However, if you have opted for a return of premium term plan, the premiums that you had paid would be returned back on maturity subject to policy terms & conditions.
What is the maximum return that I can earn from a term insurance plan?
Term insurance plans do not generate returns. They are meant to secure your finances in the face of a premature demise during the policy term. That being said, if you opt for a term plan with a return of premium option, you get returns on maturity. These returns equal the total premium paid for the coverage.
Is it possible to sell my term plan for a cash benefit?
No, term insurance plans cannot be sold for a cash benefit. However, you can surrender the policy before the stipulated tenure. In the case of basic term plans, no benefit is paid on such surrender. However, under Term Plans with Return of Premium option, you might get a cash value when you surrender the plan depending on policy terms and conditions.
Is it possible to get the premium back during the free-look period?
Yes, you get the premium refunded if you cancel the policy during the free-look period. However, the premium is refunded after deducting some costs relating to issuing the policy, cost for providing coverage upto the date of cancellation, among other deductions.
Is it possible to convert a basic term plan to Term Plan with Return of Premium option (TROP) for money-back benefits?
No, usually, basic term plans do not allow any conversion option wherein you can convert the policy to a TROP to get the money-back benefit. You would have to choose the type of policy at the time of buying the plan. Mid-term conversion is not allowed.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. output.final_text:
Do You Get Money Back After Term Life Insurance?
Term insurance plans are protection-oriented plans that are known to pay no maturity benefit. However, this is not always the case. If you need a maturity benefit along with a comprehensive scope of protection, there are term plan options that you can explore. So, let’s understand.
Can You Get Money Back After Term Insurance?
Yes, you can. The return of premium term insurance plan, is a type of term plan that returns you the premium on maturity.
As such, with the return of premium term insurance plans, you get your money back when the plan matures, and you survive the policy term, subject to policy terms & conditions.
Let’s analyse both types of term plans, one that doesn’t pay any money back and the other that does.
Type 1: Term Policies That Do Not Offer Money-Back - Basic Term Insurance
A basic term insurance plan is one that covers only death risk during the term of the policy. It does not pay any benefit if you survive the policy term and the plan matures.
Some of the features of a basic term insurance Policy are:
● The life Cover. You can opt for a high sum assured at pocket-friendly premium rates. Moreover, modern- day term plans allow comprehensive coverage with inbuilt riders (at nominal extra cost) and the option to avail of whole life coverage. ● Premium payment flexibility The premium of the policy can be paid in a single instalment, for a limited tenure or throughout the policy tenure, depending on your suitability. Thus, you get flexible premium payment tenures that help you afford the term plan without any hassle. ● You can save on tax The premiums paid for the policy qualify for a deduction up to Rs.1.5 lakhs under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961. So, you can reduce your tax liability with term insurance premiums and save tax subject to provisions stated in the Act. ● You can tune the coverage as per your needs by adding optional riders Optional riders are available under basic term plans. You can choose one or more riders on payment of nominal additional premium and adjust the coverage as per your needs.
Type 2: Term Policies That Offer Money-Back
Term insurance With Return of Premium policies are term plans that return the premium when the policy matures..
Some of the features of Term Plans with Return of Premium option are as follows –
● Life Cover Term Insurance plans with Return of Premiums too offer coverage for the chosen sum assured ● Premium return The primary benefit of a Term insurance With Return of Premium s the maturity benefit. The aggregate premium paid during the policy tenure is returned back if no death benefit is paid
during the policy term. As such, you enjoy the financial security of insurance protection, failing which you get back the premiums after the policy tenure, subject to terms & conditions of the policy. ● Optional riders There are optional riders available with the policy. You can choose to add the riders to the coverage on payment of nominal premium and enhance it for an all-inclusive scope of protection. On maturity, however, the rider premium is not refunded back. ● Tax benefits Like basic term plans, the premium paid for such policies are also eligible for a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 subject to provisions stated therein. You get a deduction up to a sum of Rs.1.5 lakhs. The survival or death benefit received through the policy is tax-exempt under Section 10 (10 D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Term Insurance Premium Calculator
Both basic term insurance plan and Term insurance With Return of Premium plans have affordable premiums since they aim to secure you against the risk of premature death. You can also calculate the expected premium of these policies using a term insurance premium calculator.
A calculator is an online tool that helps you find the estimated premium of the policy based on the details that you enter into the calculator, i.e., your age, the sum assured, tenure, etc.
How does term insurance with a return of premium work?
Under a Term Insurance with Return of Premium option, you choose the sum assured, policy tenure and premium paying term and frequency. Based on these details, your age, medical condition, etc., the premium of the policy is calculated.
You pay the premium through the tenure that you choose. In the case of death during the policy tenure, the sum assured would be paid, provided all due premium are paid, and the policy would be terminated. On the other hand, if you survive the policy tenure, the total premium that you paid during the policy term (excluding GST and the rider premium paid if any) would be refunded back in a lump sum.
Let’s understand with an example –
Mr Rakesh buys a term plan with a return of premium option for a sum assured of Rs.20 lakhs. He chooses a term of 20 years and pays regular annual premiums throughout the policy tenure. The annual premium works out to Rs.25,000.
Scenario 1 - Mr Rakesh dies during the policy term
In this case, the sum assured of Rs.20 lakhs would be paid to the nominee as a death benefit. After the payment of the death benefit, the coverage would be terminated.
Scenario 2: Mr Rakesh survives until the end of the policy term
If Mr Rakesh survives for 20 years and the policy matures, the premium will be returned. Over the tenure, Mr Rakesh paid a total premium of Rs.5 lakhs (Rs.25,000 * 20). Thus, on maturity, the premium paid would be refunded back to him, post deduction of applicable taxes and charges, if any, and the policy would be terminated.
Getting Your Money Back Within the Free-Look Period
There’s a free-look period in term insurance plans, in both basic and Term insurance With Return of Premium option The period is 15 days from the date of receipt of the policy bond by the policyholder. In the case of electronic policies and policies sourced through distance marketing mode, the period is 30 days.
The free-look period allows you to cancel the coverage if you are not satisfied with the terms and conditions of the policy. If, after buying the policy, you see that the plan is not what you expected, you can cancel the plan and seek a premium refund during the freelook period.
Cancellation of policy during the free-look period allows a refund of the premium. The insurer will refund the premium paid subject only to a deduction of a proportionate risk premium for the period of cover and the expenses incurred by the insurer on medical examination of the proposer and stamp duty charges etc.
Final Word
If you seek the benefit of comprehensive coverage along with a maturity benefit, you can opt for a return of premium term insurance plans. Such plans give you your money back if the plan matures and you survive till the maturity date, and all due premiums are paid.
So, assess your needs and then consider investing in a basic term plan or a Term Plan with Return of Premium option to enjoy financial security.
FAQs
1. If I outlive the term of the term plan, would I be eligible to get a maturity benefit?
If you have opted for a basic term insurance plan, you will not get any maturity benefit if you outlive the policy tenure. However, if you have opted for a return of premium term plan, the premiums that you had paid would be returned back on maturity subject to policy terms & conditions. 2. What is the maximum return that I can earn from a term insurance plan?
Term insurance plans do not generate returns. They are meant to secure your finances in the face of a premature demise during the policy term. That being said, if you opt for a term plan with a return of premium option, you get returns on maturity. These returns equal the total premium paid for the coverage. 3. Is it possible to get the premium back during the free-look period?
Yes, you get the premium refunded if you cancel the policy during the free-look period. However, the premium is refunded after deducting some costs relating to issuing the policy, cost for providing coverage upto the date of cancellation, among other deductions.
